title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building local AI agents using Odysseus (a workspace agent) and OpenCode (a build agent), running Gemma 4 locally via Ollama on consumer hardware. The tutorial shows wiring frontier models as fallback providers and using local models for task execution, with practical examples of agent memory, skills, scheduling, and code generation.
writeup: The tutorial walks through building local AI agents with two tools: Odysseus as a workspace agent and OpenCode as a build agent, with Gemma 4 running locally through Ollama on consumer hardware. It demonstrates a hybrid setup where local models handle task execution and frontier models are wired in as fallback providers. Concrete takeaways cover agent memory, skills, scheduling, and code generation, plus the orchestration that routes work between local and remote models.
